title: Small Context Units
one_liner: Keep files, classes and functions small enough that an agent can hold one in mind without spilling.
problem: 1500-line files, 40-method classes and 200-line functions are review-hostile for humans and context-hostile for agents. Loading one such unit can consume most of the working budget; partial loads break understanding; edits ripple unpredictably because the unit does too many things.
solution: Set explicit size budgets and enforce them with linting: e.g. files ≤ 300 lines, functions ≤ 40, classes ≤ 7 public methods. Treat exceedance as a refactor signal, not a style nit. Aim for units a competent reader can hold in mind in one pass — that is also the unit an agent can change safely.
